Indiana trailing 79-72 after yielding a 39-point 3Q from Chicago.

items.title: A rough finish to the half for the Fever, who lead 46-40.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
A rough finish to the half for the Fever, who lead 46-40.

Fever led by 18 with 3:46 left in the half — then it was a Sky 14-2 run over the final 3mins. Successful challenge by Chicago sparked it.

Opportunities are there for Fever, they were sloppy and disconnected.

items.author: Tommy Beer

🚨 New Words 🚨

The Night the Impossible Happened

The Knicks refused to quit. New York refused to stop believing.

They were down 29, staring into the abyss. 

Now they are on the brink of immortality. 

One win away from forever.

items.title: The Sky opened by playing through Kamilla Cardoso, but Indiana grabbed three offensive rebounds on its first possession and Chicago later missed 12 straight shots.

The Sky opened by playing through Kamilla Cardoso, but Indiana grabbed three offensive rebounds on its first possession and Chicago later missed 12 straight shots.

That's a difficult formula to overcome.

items.title: Hollinger POTD Kerry Kittles. Villanova SG drafted 8th by NJ in 96. Peaked at 17 ppg w/good D. After good start, missed 00-01 w/knee but  came back to be starter from 01-04. Traded to LAC in 04, where he played only 11 games before retiring at age 31. Good player when healthy.

items.title: Late Wednesday night, after the Spurs had suffered unmitigated heartbreak at the Garden, Keldon Johnson approached each player remaining in the locker room and whispered a single word:
items.author: Jeff McDonald
items.body_excerpt:
Late Wednesday night, after the Spurs had suffered unmitigated heartbreak at the Garden, Keldon Johnson approached each player remaining in the locker room and whispered a single word:

"Believe."

Belief is all the Spurs have left. It still might not be enough.

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: One word sums up the Spurs' approach to a do-or-die Game 5: 'Believe.'
media.description: Down 3-1 in the NBA Finals after blowing an historic lead in Game 4, the Spurs' season is on life support as series shifts back to San Antonio.

items.title: When OG Anunoby was 8, he begged his father, a finance professor, for a basketball hoop.
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
When OG Anunoby was 8, he begged his father, a finance professor, for a basketball hoop.

Dr. Anunoby asked his son what sort of return he expected on his investment.

OG's pitch: buy it, and I'll practice every single day. 20 years later, the returns are still compounding.

(via Dovi Safier)
#NBA
